videos.title: Bitcoin hits all-time-high briefly surpassing $68K, ethereum also rising
videos.text: welcome back to yahoo finance live time now for our crypto corner and thank goodness we got bitcoin and ethereum at record highs we even have the laggards litecoin up 22 cardano up 10 and lots of headlines today we want to get to emily parker she is the coindesk global macro editor and also the coindesk tv anchor thanks for joining us today uh lots of headlines to focus on tim cook likes crypto so does amc but can we just talk about the price movements here and what they mean sure i mean this is obviously a lot is happening at crypto at the moment bitcoin ether crossing all-time highs um you know part of this is is writing the momentum of the first bitcoin features etf from the united states that was definitely i think one of the main factors behind this initial rally but now it's kind of taking on a life of its own um and as you said you know rising tide kind of lifts all boats a lot of the other coins are rising as well so there's just a lot of energy in the market um it's interesting though because there are some regulatory actions happening in the united states that would not be seen as positive and yet you know the price doesn't seem to care and wanted to ask you switch gears a little bit in that landmark infrastructure bill that we just got was uh you know a provision that was included to amend a tax code that many in the crypto industry wanted to push back in fact that's why the bill was held up in the senate uh because there was debate over it can you discuss what that is sure so there's actually several controversial provisions in the infrastructure bill and the crypto industry really lobbied hard to get them changed but they weren't changed the bill is now waiting waiting for the signature of president biden and so the two controversial provisions one basically expands the definition of a broker which could include people like miners and the problem with this is that miners don't traditionally have customers so the short story is that it makes it difficult for them to comply with this tax reporting requirement and some crypto advocates have warned that if these tax reporting requirements are not something that one can comply with this could lead people in the crypto industry to actually exit the united states um the second controversial provision which is one that i think has gotten less attention basically says that if you receive um over ten thousand dollars worth of digital assets you have to verify the information of the sender including their social security number and report it um this is problematic obviously for a lot of reasons and so i guess the the possible silver lining is that the crypto advocates have not given up they are still trying to change this these wouldn't take effect right away so there is still time to maybe change some of these provisions that the industry finds so problematic well emily with bitcoin at record highs here a lot of hotelers like to say that but we haven't seen the same upside momentum that we have in previous advances um a lot of people were calling for 75 100 000 haven't seen that just yet i'm wondering how much of it is necessary for to get the institutionalization of uh crypto of cryptocurrencies back into wall street back into the forefront i was reading only earlier today a headline jp morgan leads wall street banks in blockchain hiring they have over 100 open positions there so putting all of this together do we have the momentum for wall street to push bitcoin to record highs in a material way well i mean i think wall street and institutional adoption and mainstream adoption has played a big role in pushing bitcoin up this far there's been news from you know it's kind of ironic because you know bitcoin sort of started as almost an alternative to wall street you know it was supposed to be independent from banks and yet you know the the in the involvement of the institutional players is definitely having a positive impact on the price but you know i think like it's it's really anybody's guess i mean you know there's it's always very difficult to tie one causal factor to the rise of bitcoin i think the only one that i have seen in the past few months that's been really clear is the bitcoin futures etf that clearly had an impact on price because that was clearly seen as a kind of you know more widespread acceptance of bitcoin and kind of an opening to a new class of investors but other than that it's really you know anyone's guess and you also see a lot of counter-intuitive trends for example you know china has really cracked down on on bitcoin even more than before and it's really as you can see it's not really affecting the price in any significant way yeah china's definitely going in the other direction we won't have to leave it there emily parker coindesk global macro editor and coindesk tv anchor thank you so much for your time and your insight today

videos.title: Why is Bitcoin at an all-time high? | BBC News
videos.text: Bitcoin surging to its first record high may have just hit a new all-time high this morning Bitcoin hit an all-time high in about a month or so we're going to have uh what people in the Bitcoin space known as a havening and in the past when there have been happenings the last one was in 2020 um we've seen the price of Bitcoin go significantly [Music] upward in many Western countries the government sets their monetary policy and overwhelmingly they like adding more money to the money supply however Bitcoin was created as a reaction to that and and the creator of Bitcoin wanted a way to control the money supply and so what he did is that he set a max number of Bitcoin that will ever be created in the past 5 to 10 years we've seen uh traditional Financial um groups like investment Banks and hedge funds and that sort of thing invest more in Bitcoin a lot of people trade cryptocurrencies based on senent and so when you have all of these really exciting news stories about uh the price of Bitcoin skyrocketing and governments starting to accept cryptocurrencies more then you can see with that positive sentiment people trading more Bitcoin so in the past cryptocurrency mining has been dominated by miners in China and now uh China's starting to pull out of the cryptocurrency mining game and so it's not clear how Bitcoin mining will change after the happening there might be a change in who mins to make it financially viable and nobody really knows what the effects of that could be

videos.text: hey guys what's going on interacts capital and welcome back to the channel today we're going to be focusing on bitcoin's price actions through technical analysis but then we're going to be diving a little bit deeper we're going to be comparing why this current all-time high that we saw last week 67 000 why this all-time high is different to the one we saw back here in late april and we're going to be diving into that in a bit more detail looking under the hood of bitcoin's price action using on-chain analytics just to give you that perspective in the macro sense but also on chain downside wicking just to tap into that buy side liquidity and great u-shaped reversal right over here would be fantastic to see a weekly candle close above this potential sideways range that would be effectively a breakout from the weekly ball flag that's all that's what we're looking for that weekly confirmation that we've been able to test this support area and break out from this weekly sideways channel and that's going to be a good sign towards eclipsing these highs also which we're currently under at the moment but weekly close above 63 000 and there's a good chance we could revisit the 65 regions and beyond so at the moment so far so good this retest for bitcoin has been successful thus far but we're gonna be looking at the global in and out of the money when it comes to on-chain analytics and i'd just like to quickly thank into the block for these insights you can see right over here almost 100 profitability on bitcoin in all of bitcoin's history 97.95 that's the profitability of bitcoin investors so it's definitely been a good investment to allocate a portion of your cash position to bitcoin that's a very positive metric to always keep an eye out we're going to soon reach 100 1000 100 profitability for bitcoin but what's even more interesting is that we're seeing renewed institutional interest and also retail interest because if you just focus here on the total buy side volume transaction volume in usd that recent breakout to 67k was met with fantastic volume almost 750 billion dollars worth of transactional volume and you can see that that peak far exceeds any peak we saw in september in august july may april so you can see a tremendous increase in that transactional volume in this recent break out to 67k whereas back here in april may the volume was below way below substantially below 250 billion so you can see how there is a tremendous difference in the transactional volume and us the bitcoin across time april very small amounts of volume compared to what we're seeing at that recent peak so renewed buy side interest is definitely apparent on chain and before we move on to the next part it's really important to just emphasize this it's a 7x difference in volume which is only a great indication of buy side confidence and interest and exuberance towards bitcoin as an asset and we're also seeing less speculation so this is how much money is occupied by traders and you can see that october of course is a little bit higher than september but overall the speculation back here for instance earlier this year to may for instance that speculation was much higher traders were very active in the market right over here you can see how the amount of trading activity the amount of traders in the market was really high compared to what we're seeing right now at these levels so comparing to earlier of this year and essentially this recent quarter and end of q3 you're seeing a tremendous decline in the amount of speculation going on via trading activity short-term speculation that's inherent in just traders taking positions and selling for swings or scalp trading you can see that there is a diminishing by 40 percent when we compare april to october and that's a tremendous change in terms of trading activity so there's more retail and institutional interest in the transactional volume but there's also less speculation so holders are more confident to be holding and traders themselves aren't necessarily eager to be actually actively trading in the market they want to see what's going to happen with price at all time highs right now traders want to see what happens so that they can now or at least a little bit later on decide on what their strategy is going to be but at the moment they're not rushing to be trading they're happy with what's going on at the moment no speculation because there is a bit more confidence skewing towards holding positions rather than trading them for swings or scalp trading and lastly let's talk about the funding rate you can see that the funding rate here compared to back in earlier of this year dating to as far back to january you can see that the funding rate was quite high especially after february mid-february and then mid-march especially mid-april a bit in may but we haven't seen such high funding rates many many months really so essentially the first half of the year is where we saw high positive funding rates which often preceded crashes and corrections whereas now we're seeing these funding rates in the negative territory so really interesting to see that if anything if any takeaway should be from funding is that right now we're seeing negative funding as opposed to positive funding and the markets overall are just less overheated compared to when we saw the markets overheated and experiencing quite a few crashes near that major resistance error right this major resistance area if you were just to compare these points of contact in this major resistance error and just juxtapose that with funding you'd see a high correlation between high funding rates and these pullbacks occurring upon rejection from this major resistance error so really important to just pay attention to that because that previous resistance with positive funding rates were essentially ingredients for a pullback whereas now that previous resistance has flipped into a support and now you're seeing really low funding rates which is indicating a less overheated market so the context macro wise in terms of price action analysis and in terms of on-chain analysis as well two different stories less overheated market more overheated market going back here previous major resistance error now acting as a new major area of demand in any case guys thank you so much for watching this video leave a like if you'd like to see more on chain analysis by into the blog thank you to enter the blog for these terrific insights that really gave you some perspective in understanding bitcoin's price action on the macro perspective really helpful insights and of course subscribe to the future i'm rex capital and i'll speak to you in the next one speak to you soon
